items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, one of San José's key commercial corridors. This represents significant economic activity in the area.

items.headline: City commissions reveal 2026-27 priorities in rare public work session
items.summary: Saratoga City Council met with five commissions (planning, parks, traffic safety, library/arts, and heritage preservation) to discuss and approve their annual work plans. This public session offers insight into what city commissions will focus on over the next year, from traffic improvements to parks development to historic preservation initiatives.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering an amnesty program to help businesses get compliant with licensing requirements, paired with outreach to shopping centers. This could affect local business owners who may have fallen behind on renewals or permits.

items.headline: City announces retail and small business economic development incentives
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out new incentive programs targeting retail, small businesses, and startups as part of broader economic development efforts. Residents interested in local business vitality and the health of Cupertino's commercial districts should track what these incentives entail.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up designated as annual city event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an annual city-sponsored special event on March 25, authorizing city funds and allowing donations for the cleanup initiative.

items.headline: Housing commission reviews federal community development grants
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applications for federal Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These grants support affordable housing and community development projects that directly benefit Sunnyvale residents.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area planning study to examine land use and transportation
items.summary: The Planning Commission is launching a study of the San Antonio Road area to evaluate different land use and transportation options. This could affect how the area develops and how residents and commuters move through it.

items.headline: New retail vitality rules approved to strengthen neighborhood shopping districts
items.summary: The Commission recommended changes to zoning and building codes designed to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. These amendments aim to support and revitalize commercial areas across Palo Alto.
